title: Red Hairing

jokes.text: I'd give $20,000 for a lemonade right now. For the most part.
jokes.setup: Narrator states Lindsay has adopted a new value system in the desert
jokes.punchline: She immediately demonstrates she hasn't changed at all by valuing a lemonade at $20k
jokes.explanation: Perfect narrator misdirection followed by immediate contradiction. 'For the most part' is beautifully economical.

jokes.text: Marky! Mommy needs mescaline!
jokes.setup: Lindsay calling for her activist boyfriend
jokes.punchline: She casually demands hallucinogens like it's grocery shopping
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's privileged approach to counterculture - even her drug use sounds entitled.

jokes.text: It's not DeBrie, Ma Bark. It's Lindsay. I just cut off my hair.
jokes.setup: Ma Bark confuses Lindsay for DeBrie
jokes.punchline: Even after cutting her hair, Lindsay is so generic she's mistaken for another character
jokes.explanation: Great callback that reinforces Lindsay's lack of distinctive personality even when trying to change.

jokes.text: I know who you are. Now start shoveling! God, you're lazier than Lindsay.
jokes.setup: Ma Bark says she knows who Lindsay is
jokes.punchline: Then immediately compares her unfavorably to herself
jokes.explanation: Nice quick reversal - knows exactly who she is but still insults her.

jokes.text: You really are color-blind. Face-blind.
jokes.setup: Marky makes a racial comment about separating whites from browns
jokes.punchline: Lindsay corrects his disability type, revealing he meant literal colors not races
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - audience thinks he's being racist but he literally can't see faces or colors properly.

jokes.text: The strong will become the weak. But I think I overdid it first on the maca, because I ended up seeing two of him.
jokes.setup: Marky delivers an intimidating threat to the CEOs
jokes.punchline: Undermines it by admitting he was too high to see straight
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of tough guy posturing with hippie drug confusion.

jokes.text: That lizard bastard bit me! God! Who keeps numbing these desert animals?
jokes.setup: Marky is upset about killing an innocent iguana
jokes.punchline: Immediately calls it a bastard when it bites him, then blames someone for drugging animals
jokes.explanation: Fast character flip from animal lover to calling it names, plus absurd drug paranoia.

jokes.text: Put some rubbing mescaline on it!
jokes.setup: Marky needs first aid for a lizard bite
jokes.punchline: Ma Bark suggests rubbing mescaline instead of rubbing alcohol
jokes.explanation: Simple malapropism - functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: It's Lindsay's Arrested Development.
jokes.setup: The typical opening credits sequence
jokes.punchline: This episode is specifically Lindsay's version of the show title
jokes.explanation: Clever meta twist on the show title that fits the season's character-focused structure.

jokes.text: Come on, Cindy. / What do you think? / Ugh. Smells weird in here. What is that? / I think it's just... not urine.
jokes.setup: They're moving into what should be a luxury penthouse
jokes.punchline: The best thing they can say about the smell is that it's NOT urine
jokes.explanation: Good low bar humor - when 'not urine' is a selling point, you know it's bad.

jokes.text: Cindy? You okay? Look, Lindsay, Cindy already marked the master.
jokes.setup: They're deciding sleeping arrangements in the penthouse
jokes.punchline: The ostrich has claimed the master bedroom by urinating in it
jokes.explanation: Absurd household dynamics where the bird gets the best room by default.

jokes.text: One year later, however, the only thing they'd actually changed was getting the 'not urine' smell out of the penthouse.
jokes.setup: Time jump suggesting they've been activists for a year
jokes.punchline: Their only accomplishment was basic hygiene
jokes.explanation: Good callback that emphasizes their complete lack of actual activism progress.

jokes.text: You're looking at Lindsay. Still Lindsay.
jokes.setup: Documentary-style talking head introduction
jokes.punchline: She emphasizes she hasn't changed despite a year of supposed activism
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Lindsay's proud of not growing or changing at all.

jokes.text: How was beg? / Beg was good. I found sometres lechescake.
jokes.setup: Casual check-in about Lindsay's day
jokes.punchline: She was literally begging for food and mispronounces 'tres leches cake'
jokes.explanation: Shows how far Lindsay has fallen while maintaining her obliviousness to other cultures.

jokes.text: I'll, uh, change out of these filthy clothes and into some filthy lingerie. I don't think I washed it.
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to be romantic
jokes.punchline: Even her attempt at seduction involves dirty clothes
jokes.explanation: Shows their squalid living conditions affecting even intimate moments.

jokes.text: Look, look, look. Am I guilty of anything? Yes. Of having a great sense of humor.
jokes.setup: Herbert Love addressing harassment allegations
jokes.punchline: Claims his only crime is being too funny
jokes.explanation: Typical politician deflection that's both arrogant and tone-deaf.

jokes.text: I forgot the joke.
jokes.setup: Herbert offers to tell the joke to prove his innocence
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he can't remember it
jokes.explanation: Perfect puncturing of his defense - can't even remember his own 'hilarious' joke.

jokes.text: Did you take the silver glitter? / Nope. I mean, I'd say check the junk drawer, but I can't differentiate anymore.
jokes.setup: Marky looking for bomb supplies
jokes.punchline: Lindsay can't tell junk from valuables anymore
jokes.explanation: Shows how far Lindsay's standards have fallen - everything looks like junk to her now.

jokes.text: Blue in the face, yeah. That's better than what I had, actually. I was gonna say they'd be covered in ink and glitter.
jokes.setup: Marky explaining his paint bomb plan
jokes.punchline: Lindsay accidentally makes a better pun than Marky's literal description
jokes.explanation: Lindsay accidentally outsmarts Marky with wordplay she doesn't even realize she made.

jokes.text: Does it look like I'm lying? It did. But not to a man with face-blindness.
jokes.setup: Lindsay denies going to see Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: She's obviously lying but Marky can't tell because of his condition
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of established character trait to enable plot point - classic AD layered storytelling.

jokes.text: Because Lindsay could only spend so many hours with a shrill, feathered creature... ...and needed to get away.
jokes.setup: Describing Lindsay's need to escape
jokes.punchline: Cuts to her with Lucille 2, revealing who the 'shrill, feathered creature' actually is
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - audience assumes it's about the ostrich but it's about Ma Bark or maybe even Lucille 2.

jokes.text: I used to wear that with the Captain. / Tennille? / And not make contact, yes!
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 mentions wearing something with 'the Captain'
jokes.punchline: References Captain and Tennille duo with a no-contact rule twist
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference with a twist about their professional relationship.

jokes.text: There are two S's in 'congress.' And I just call myself Lucille... Lucille 1. I'm so sorry. No. Just Lucille.
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 correcting Lindsay's poster
jokes.punchline: Lindsay keeps making the name more complicated when trying to simplify it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lindsay's overthinking of simple corrections.

jokes.text: It's from a dear Hispanic supporter. And I'll never get it into the bay in one piece.
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 offering cake from a supporter
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions disposing of it in the bay like a body
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that sounds like disposing of evidence while just talking about cake.

jokes.text: Honestly, Lucille 2, you've been like a mother to me. Aw. Except kind and loving and willing to let me eat.
jokes.setup: Lindsay gives Lucille 2 a sweet compliment
jokes.punchline: Immediately contrasts it with her real mother's terrible parenting
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat that uses series history to land a joke about Lucille's parenting.

jokes.text: That's a face? Everything I do is wrong.
jokes.setup: Lindsay catches Marky licking what he thinks is cake
jokes.punchline: He realizes he was licking a photo of a person due to his face-blindness
jokes.explanation: Excellent use of established character trait for gross-out humor that feels earned.

jokes.text: I don't know if I've said this before- it's not important or anything- but I'm really, really pretty.
jokes.setup: Lindsay feeling ignored by her face-blind boyfriend
jokes.punchline: States the most important thing about her identity while claiming it's not important
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay moment - her vanity is so core to her identity she can't hide it even when trying to be humble.

jokes.text: I'm the straightest guy you know! Why does every man feel like they have to say that to me?
jokes.setup: Marky defending his attraction to Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Lindsay notes this is a pattern with the men in her life
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Lindsay's history of dating men who protest their straightness too much.

jokes.text: Ooh, a Neiman's catalog. But it was what she found stuck inside the catalog that got her attention.
jokes.setup: Lindsay distracted by shopping catalog during argument
jokes.punchline: Finds something even more interesting hidden inside
jokes.explanation: Classic Lindsay - gets distracted by shopping even during relationship drama.

jokes.text: And that's when she found the mother lode. 'From Gangie 4: Facelift.' What a load of... Mother.
jokes.setup: Lindsay finds check from grandmother with unusual memo
jokes.punchline: Starts to say 'load of crap' but changes to 'mother' seeing family connection
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with character-appropriate language modification upon discovering family involvement.

jokes.text: Oh, great, looks like we've got another high-end hooker in here. Oh, thank you, Mother.
jokes.setup: Lucille making a snide comment about Lindsay's appearance
jokes.punchline: Lindsay takes it as a compliment about looking expensive
jokes.explanation: Good mother-daughter dynamic - Lucille's insult backfires because Lindsay takes it as praise.

jokes.text: Put a 'Y' on there. 'Nosy.' And you should be thanking me for that. You looked like a can opener.
jokes.setup: Lucille explaining why she wrote 'nosy' on a nose job check
jokes.punchline: Compares Lindsay's original nose to a kitchen utensil
jokes.explanation: Classic Lucille cruelty with a specific, memorable visual comparison.

jokes.text: I'll be out on parole by the time you work up a tear.
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to manipulate Lucille with emotion
jokes.punchline: Lucille's completely unmoved and points out how slowly Lindsay cries
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - completely immune to manipulation and insults Lindsay's emotional range.

jokes.text: Just goes to show, the apple does not far from the tree fall. I am like you nothing.
jokes.setup: Lucille comparing herself to Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Lindsay mangles the idiom while trying to deny the similarity
jokes.explanation: Lindsay's mangled syntax proves Lucille's point while trying to deny it.

jokes.text: Your 3:00 p.m. hot rock massage is open. Thanks for coming, sweetheart.
jokes.setup: Emotional mother-daughter confrontation in prison
jokes.punchline: Lucille's 'hard time' includes spa treatments
jokes.explanation: Perfect puncturing of Lucille's claims about doing 'hard time' - she's living better than most.

jokes.text: What's the worst that can happen? [Cut to chaos with ostrich]
jokes.setup: Marky confident about leaving Cindy locked up
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to ostrich causing havoc
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff - though the structure is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Ah! What a great day to step out onto an over-irrigated golf course and play a game that wastes vast acres of usable, farm-able land.
jokes.setup: Politician making public appearance at golf event
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastically describes golf in environmentally critical terms
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing politician's tone-deaf relationship with environmental issues.

jokes.text: Lindsay, I forgot, you dyed your hair. Do you have any money?
jokes.setup: Father seeing daughter for first time in a year
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for money after minimal pleasantries
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment - completely self-absorbed and gets right to what he needs.

jokes.text: I tried. Tell your therapist I tried.
jokes.setup: Maeby lying about getting a lifetime achievement award
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives up on the lie and pre-emptively defends herself to future therapy
jokes.explanation: Great Maeby moment - her cynicism extends to pre-emptively defending her parenting to future therapists.

jokes.text: I'm getting a lifetime achievement award for my work in the entertainment business. I tried. Tell your therapist I tried.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asks what Maeby is doing at event
jokes.punchline: Maeby gives up on honesty immediately after narrator praised it
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure - immediately subverts narrator's claim about honesty with meta-commentary about giving up.

jokes.text: What's your name, princess? Cindy Featherbottom. Of the Laguna Beach Featherbottoms?
jokes.setup: Herbert hitting on Lindsay at the event
jokes.punchline: Lindsay uses the name from Tobias's Mrs. Featherbottom persona
jokes.explanation: Great deep callback to Tobias's British nanny persona that fans will catch.

jokes.text: It's, uh, 714... Right this way, sir, right this way.
jokes.setup: Lindsay giving Herbert her number
jokes.punchline: Gets interrupted before finishing, showing her losing track of time
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Cinderella parallel with her losing track of time.

jokes.text: Oh, God, he blued himself.
jokes.setup: Seeing Marky covered in blue paint from the failed bomb
jokes.punchline: References Tobias's blue man group aspirations
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that connects two storylines - Marky's paint bomb and Tobias's blue phase.

jokes.text: Give me a call; perhaps we can engage in some sexual congress. Although that's a total giveaway, right?
jokes.setup: Herbert hitting on Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Uses 'congress' sexually, then acknowledges it reveals his political identity
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with political terminology, though Herbert pointing it out weakens the joke.

jokes.text: In fairness to Lindsay, it was Marky's face-blindness that led him to mistake this photo for the candidate. Why Lindsay failed to recognize musical icon Nat King Cole cannot be explained.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Lindsay didn't recognize Herbert Love
jokes.punchline: Shows Marky mistook Nat King Cole for Love, while Lindsay has no excuse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reveal that explains the plot hole while creating new comedy about Lindsay's ignorance.

jokes.text: Are you really going to whore yourself out like that? I am not a whore. Yeah, you're a whore. I am not a whore.
jokes.setup: Maeby calling out Lindsay's plan
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's vehement denials sound increasingly uncertain
jokes.explanation: Functional comedy through repetition but not particularly inspired.

jokes.text: If you see a woman named Lindsay, tell her to bail me out. I'll do my best, sir.
jokes.setup: Marky asking a random person for help
jokes.punchline: He's talking directly to Lindsay but can't recognize her
jokes.explanation: Great use of established character trait for situational irony.

jokes.text: Gentlemen, start your engines. No, Gob. Oh!
jokes.setup: Lindsay feeling good about turning a man's head
jokes.punchline: Buster crashes into the ostrich warning because his head was turned
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that deflates Lindsay's moment of confidence.

jokes.text: Kids or people who like kids. So, where's Marky?
jokes.setup: Maeby explaining why someone can't visit
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions restriction about pedophiles then changes subject
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually, typical of the show's edgy comedy.

jokes.text: They're upping my charges from prank bomb to non-Arab terrorism.
jokes.setup: Marky explaining his legal troubles
jokes.punchline: The distinction between types of terrorism charges
jokes.explanation: Dark political humor about terrorism classifications, feels a bit cheap.

jokes.text: Jail's a lot like Swappigans, except there's only one thing to swap. It's scary.
jokes.setup: Marky trying to explain prison life
jokes.punchline: References hippie bartering culture then realizes the dark implication
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific way to reference prison sexual violence without being explicit.

jokes.text: Anus tart. Yep, that's me.
jokes.setup: Marky describing himself in prison context
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'a new start' to sound like a sexual prison term
jokes.explanation: Callback to Tobias's unfortunate license plate choice with prison context.

jokes.text: Meet, greet. Sure are sweet. What a treat. What did the lamb say? Baa? Bleat.
jokes.setup: Herbert trying to be charming with wordplay
jokes.punchline: Gets increasingly awkward with forced rhyming
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that's supposed to show Herbert's bad pickup style.

jokes.text: Your lips are like a Murphy bed. They don't take up much space, but they are there when you need 'em.
jokes.setup: Herbert attempting a romantic compliment
jokes.punchline: Compares her lips to furniture in the most unromantic way possible
jokes.explanation: Absurdly specific and unromantic comparison that's perfectly in character for a tone-deaf politician.

jokes.text: I'm going to meet with some of my, uh, 'constititutes.'
jokes.setup: Herbert explaining where he's going
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'constituents' in way that suggests prostitutes
jokes.explanation: Good political wordplay with sexual innuendo - economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: The wall between Mexico and the U.S.? Tell him to take it down? Or just not to put it up. No wall yet. Oh, no, there's no- I knew that, yeah.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining border wall issue
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's confusion about current state of wall construction
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Lindsay's political ignorance and face-saving lies.

jokes.text: The wall between Mexico and the U.S.? Tell him to take it down? Or just not to put it up. No wall yet. Oh, no, there's no- I knew that, yeah.
jokes.setup: Michael giving Lindsay political advice
jokes.punchline: Lindsay doesn't realize the wall doesn't exist yet
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's political ignorance and Michael's similar confusion about current events.

jokes.text: Fat Tracey or thin Tracey at the end?
jokes.setup: Michael asking about Lindsay's romantic interest
jokes.punchline: Reduces a woman to weight fluctuation timeline
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's shallow perspective on women, though the joke feels somewhat dated.

jokes.text: What the hell? It's better for my back anyway.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asking Herbert to be 'against the wall'
jokes.punchline: Herbert thinks it's a sexual position request
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that shows the miscommunication between their different goals.

jokes.text: Wait... I can't do this unless you promise to be against the wall. What the hell? It's better for my back anyway.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asking for political promise during intimate moment
jokes.punchline: Herbert thinks she's talking about sexual position
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke - political and sexual meanings of 'against the wall' create perfect miscommunication.

jokes.text: Aah, Jesus Christ! Oh! I'm sorry. Why would you do that? No, I thought it was cute, I saw it in a movie.
jokes.setup: Lindsay tries a romantic gesture with the necklace
jokes.punchline: Herbert reacts with pain and horror
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that shows Lindsay's romantic gestures going wrong.

jokes.text: Lindsay wasn't special, but he'd gotten the necklaces on special.
jokes.setup: Herbert telling Lindsay she's special
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals the jewelry was just discounted, not meaningful
jokes.explanation: Excellent narrator deflation of romantic moment with perfect wordplay.

jokes.text: Uh, Bluth. I made a reservation. Hi-lo. Nice to meet you. Oh, I'm sorry, the Bluths have been seated. But she wasn't the only Bluth pretending to be something they weren't that night.
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to get table under family name
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals multiple family members using deception simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Good setup for family convergence scene - shows typical Bluth deception habits coinciding.

jokes.text: High school. Brother and sister. We can't be brother-sister- she knows about the family. We don't. Just met.
jokes.setup: Explaining their relationship to their dates
jokes.punchline: Can't coordinate their story and create more confusion
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as they fumble the simple task of explaining their relationship.

jokes.text: Well, don't you have to have power to abuse the power?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Hollywood power abuse
jokes.punchline: Herbert suggests Michael has no power to abuse
jokes.explanation: Good burn that cuts right to Michael's insecurity about his career status.

jokes.text: Well, don't you have to have power to abuse the power?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Hollywood power abuse
jokes.punchline: Herbert's cutting observation about Michael's actual status
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment - Herbert cuts through Michael's pretensions with logical observation about power dynamics.

jokes.text: You wouldn't believe the scallops they're throwing away here, but I got out of that Dumpster my way.
jokes.setup: Marky explaining how he 'beat the system'
jokes.punchline: He's just Dumpster diving for restaurant scraps
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marky moment - his anti-establishment victory is just finding free food.

jokes.text: It's over between us. My wife found out and I told her there was nothing emotional, that you were just a prostitute. I'm not a prostitute.
jokes.setup: Herbert ending affair
jokes.punchline: Reveals he described Lindsay as prostitute to his wife
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Herbert's callous treatment - makes Lindsay's situation even more degrading.

jokes.text: It's over between us. My wife found out and I told her there was nothing emotional, that you were just a prostitute.
jokes.setup: Herbert breaking up with Lindsay
jokes.punchline: His defense to his wife makes Lindsay sound worse
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about how men minimize their affairs by dehumanizing the other woman.

jokes.text: This isn't over. I'm not some taco you can throw into the bay.
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to sound threatening and defiant
jokes.punchline: Uses the most random and unthreatening metaphor possible
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and absurd metaphor that completely undermines her attempt to be intimidating.

jokes.text: It's called hooking up. And, yeah, I got a little sidetracked, but I know who I am now. I'm back to being severely liberal.
jokes.setup: Lindsay defending herself to her mother
jokes.punchline: Corrects 'hooking' to 'hooking up' then claims to be 'severely' liberal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lindsay's superficial understanding of both sex and politics.

jokes.text: Show-and-tell? Oh, she just means these photos of Herbert Love with a mystery redhead he's cheating on his wife with. Who took these? The first two were taken by the security camera in the Albertsons parking lot.
jokes.setup: Sally revealing damaging photos of Herbert
jokes.punchline: Reveals affair was documented in mundane grocery store parking lot
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing how unromantic affair was - surveillance cameras at grocery store.

jokes.text: I've got to find someone to jump onto that stage and to show everyone here that we are the party that has complete con-con... Complete con-con-con... con-con... Complete... Giant.... trol.
jokes.setup: Campaign worker trying to project confidence
jokes.punchline: Can't say 'control' while asking someone to show party has control
jokes.explanation: Good ironic comedy - speaker demonstrates lack of control while asking for demonstration of control.

jokes.text: Love wants to put up a wall. No, no. I mean, not just any wall. A wall to keep out the Mexicans. Oh, wow, what a great crowd, uh...
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to turn crowd against Herbert Love
jokes.punchline: Accidentally promotes the exact policy she meant to criticize to supportive crowd
jokes.explanation: Excellent political comedy - Lindsay's complete misreading of audience leads to promoting what she meant to oppose.

jokes.text: I mean, someone should put up a wall. Yeah. We should put up a wall. Put up this wall. Put up this wall! Put... up... this wall.
jokes.setup: Lindsay gets caught up in the moment after being attacked
jokes.punchline: Accidentally starts chanting for the policy she came to oppose
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment where Lindsay's true nature emerges under pressure - becomes the thing she claimed to oppose.

jokes.text: My name is Lindsay Bluth, and this is who I am. A Bluth, just like her mother.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's moment of self-realization
jokes.punchline: Admits she's exactly what she tried not to be
jokes.explanation: Perfect character arc conclusion - Lindsay finally accepts her true nature instead of pretending to be someone else.

jokes.text: And Annyong tries to stick the Bluth family for a tomato juice that costs him $700 and ultimately his freedom. Good-bye, Annyong.
jokes.setup: Explaining Annyong's subplot
jokes.punchline: His expensive tomato juice leads to his downfall
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that wraps up Annyong's story with appropriate irony about his petty revenge backfiring.

tmdb_overview: Lindsay continues to push away from being a Bluth by embracing a relationship and finding a new career.
insight: Wordplay-driven episode sprawls across 77 jokes but lands softly at 75.2 impact.
